+{"id":"ft-5o6u5","title":"[security] Redactor leaves base64 secret suffixes unmasked","description":"Scope: redactor.rs secret handling.

Finding: GENERIC_API_KEY / GENERIC_TOKEN / GENERIC_SECRET only consume alnum/underscore/dash (and token dot) values, then Redactor::redact replaces only the regex match. Base64-style client secrets containing /, +, or = are partially redacted and leave trailing secret bytes visible.

Evidence: crates/frankenterm-core/src/redactor.rs:47, crates/frankenterm-core/src/redactor.rs:53, crates/frankenterm-core/src/redactor.rs:65, crates/frankenterm-core/src/redactor.rs:220.

Repro unit:
let r = Redactor::new();
let input = "client_secret=abcdEFGHijklMNOP/QRST+UVWX=YZ1234567890";
let out = r.redact(input);
assert!(!out.contains("QRST+UVWX=YZ1234567890"));

Currently the assertion fails because the output leaves the /QRST+UVWX=YZ1234567890 suffix.

Impact: robot/MCP/audit paths that rely on Redactor can persist or return the unredacted suffix of common OAuth/client/base64 secrets.

Fix: extend generic secret/token/key value patterns to consume base64-ish quoted or whitespace-delimited values, and add regression coverage for /, +, and = values.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"amber-fox","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:37:13.774888Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T19:45:03.413816Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T19:45:03.413576Z","close_reason":"Extended GENERIC_API_KEY/GENERIC_TOKEN/GENERIC_SECRET value charsets to include base64 bytes /+= so OAuth/client_secret values are fully redacted. 5 regression tests added in redactor::tests covering all three patterns + whitespace-boundary preservation + detect() span. All pass via rch (frankenterm-core --lib redactor::). Commit 652600f4.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-b677n","title":"[MEDIUM] wgpu readback poll loop has no test coverage for timeout, disconnect, or error-propagation paths","description":"Commit 6f41130c (br-43bbl fix wgpu readback polling) rewrites the
readback wait in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:168-193
from:

\`\`\`rust
self.device.poll(PollType::Wait { submission_index: None, timeout: Some(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT) })?;
receiver.recv_timeout(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT)?...
\`\`\`

to a bounded poll loop:

\`\`\`rust
let deadline = Instant::now() + WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT;
loop {
    self.device.poll(wgpu::PollType::Poll)?;
    match receiver.recv_timeout(Duration::from_millis(10)) {
        Ok(result) => { result?; break; }
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) if Instant::now() < deadline => continue,
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) => return Err(...),
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Disconnected) => return Err(...),
    }
}
\`\`\`

Concerns with the new implementation:

1. \`PollType::Poll\` is non-blocking by contract in wgpu 25. The loop
   relies on \`Poll\` actually driving the \`map_async\` callback forward.
   If a future wgpu point release changes \`Poll\` to be strictly
   \"dispatch-only\" (no callback invocation), the loop would always
   hit the 5s timeout. There is no test pinning this behavior.

2. The \`Disconnected\` branch is reachable only if the \`map_async\`
   closure drops without sending — that happens e.g., if wgpu drops
   the submission without invoking the callback (device lost, internal
   error). No test exercises this path.

3. \`Instant::now() + WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT\` panics on overflow
   (impossible in practice for 5s, but worth noting as a latent
   concern). Prefer \`checked_add\`.

4. Busy-wait cost: on the timeout path, the loop spins ~500 iterations
   (5000ms / 10ms) calling \`device.poll\` each time. The prior
   \`PollType::Wait\` blocked in the driver. This is a CPU-efficiency
   regression on cancellation/device-loss paths, not a correctness
   regression.

Impact: readback behavior on wgpu failure paths (device lost, long
delay, map_async disconnect) is silently unverified. A correctness
regression in \`PollType::Poll\` semantics between wgpu versions would
silently break screenshots and readback-based features.

Fix direction:
- Add a unit-level test with a mock/stub Device that controls
  \`poll()\` return timing and can force a Disconnected on the mpsc
  channel. Assert each of the four loop outcomes.
- Consider moving the readback loop into a small helper function
  that takes a \`Fn()\` poll closure + channel receiver, to make it
  unit-testable without a real GPU.
- If unit isolation is impractical, at minimum add an integration
  test that triggers a readback and asserts completion within a
  tight CPU budget (regression test for busy-wait blow-up).

Severity: medium — latent risk in a GPU-facing path that typically
works and has no automated coverage for failure modes.

Evidence: commit 6f41130c diff;
crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:168-193 at HEAD.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:33.419495Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:48:13.062749Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:48:13.062383Z","close_reason":"Added timeout/disconnect/cancel-style readback coverage and hardened the helper deadline with checked_add","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu","review","test-gap","testing","webgpu"]}
 {"id":"ft-b9ndv","title":"[fuzz] add MCP request params parser harness","description":"Existing fuzz coverage already hits pattern-pack TOML/binary, config TOML, workflow YAML, robot envelope JSON, IPC auth envelope, snapshot deserialization, replay recordings, and term/escape parsers. But there is still no fuzz target for MCP request-param parsing.

Evidence:
- `fuzz/Cargo.toml` has no MCP parser target; current targets stop at `ipc_auth_envelope`, `robot_envelope_json`, and related config parsers.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_types.rs:25-170` defines the externally-controlled MCP request param structs (`StateParams`, `GetTextParams`, `SearchParams`, `EventsParams`, etc.), but they are `pub(super)` and only covered by unit/proptest deserialization checks.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_types.rs:1170-1845` has property tests over many `serde_json::from_value(...)` paths, but no byte-level fuzzing against malformed/truncated/oversized JSON.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_framework.rs:203-265` also round-trips untrusted remote MCP payloads through `serde_json::from_value(...)`.

Why it matters:
- MCP is a network/external-input surface with many optional/defaulted fields and multiple tool-specific param shapes.
- Proptests cover valid structured values, not adversarial raw JSON bytes, duplicate keys, deep nesting, type confusion, or truncation.

Fix:
- Add a fuzz seam plus harness that feeds raw JSON/Value payloads through the MCP request-param decode paths (or framework payload bridge) and asserts no panic / deterministic decode outcomes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-04-23T19:26:37.331975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:48.039115Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:48.038906Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 4561eac1: add MCP request params fuzz harness and seed corpus","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","review"]}

 {"id":"ft-bkgbq","title":"[architecture][follow-up ft-zo3cr] Centralize PolicyEngine + PolicyGatedInjector + WorkflowRunner assembly in main.rs","description":"## Context

ft-zo3cr identified an architectural smell: `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs` repeats the PolicyEngine + PolicyGatedInjector + WorkflowRunner assembly in at least 4 branches with slightly-different defaults:

- :6992-6999 — PolicyEngine::new + with_tuning + with_command_gate_config + with_policy_rules (bare, for a single policy check)
- :15341-15359 — PolicyEngine::permissive + PolicyGatedInjector + CxPolicyInjector + WorkflowEngine + PaneWorkflowLockManager (full workflow runtime stack)
- :19850-19869 — PolicyEngine::new(require_prompt_active) + with_tuning + with_command_gate_config + with_policy_rules (similar to :6992 but with injector+storage)
- :25526-25540 — PolicyEngine::new(true) + with_tuning BUT OMITS with_command_gate_config and with_policy_rules (drift!)

ft-zo3cr in-scope fix (landed in ddf4eb8f) closed a similar drift in mcp_tools.rs:2558. The main.rs sites were left out because this architectural bead scoped only crates/frankenterm-core/* via its reservation.

## Why this matters

The :19850 vs :25526 comparison is the exact kind of drift the bead warns about: one call site gates on command_gate + policy_rules, the other doesn't. Any future fix to one doesn't land in the other. The :19846-19849 comment in current code already records a past regression of this shape (workflow injector diverged from the direct send path).

## Suggested direction (matches the bead's original proposal)

Introduce a single composition factory in frankenterm-core (or a dedicated module under crates/frankenterm/src/) exposing shape-typed builders:

    fn build_human_send_stack(config: &Config) -> HumanSendStack { ... }
    fn build_mcp_workflow_stack(config: &Config, storage: StorageHandle, wezterm: ...) -> McpWorkflowStack { ... }
    fn build_robot_workflow_stack(config: &Config, storage: ...) -> RobotWorkflowStack { ... }

Each returns a bundle (PolicyEngine + Injector + optional Runner). Handlers at the 4 main.rs sites become declarative — they ask for the bundle rather than open-code the assembly.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Each of the 4 main.rs sites uses one of the shared factories.

 {"id":"ft-lokam","title":"[LOW] diag bundle environment.json leaks absolute workspace cwd; tracing fields leak operator paths","description":"Found during /security-audit-for-saas pass #6 (pane 5, 2026-04-21).

## Summary
Three logging/telemetry surfaces all come out clean on the obvious
secret-leak vectors (no bearer-token / API-key / pane-content
fields in tracing calls; no /etc/ or .ssh/ reads in diag
collection). One defense-in-depth gap remains: path privacy.

## Findings

### F1 [LOW] diag bundle environment.json includes absolute cwd
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/diagnostic.rs:87-89
  ```rust
  cwd: std::env::current_dir()
      .ok()
      .map(|p| p.display().to_string()),
  ```
- Diag bundles (`ft diag bundle`) are often attached to bug reports
  and shared outside the host. An absolute cwd reveals:
  - the user's home path segment (`/Users/<username>/...` or
    `/home/<username>/...`),
  - the project path (`/Users/alice/projects/secret-startup-pitch-deck/`),
  - on some setups, mount points that hint at filesystem layout.
- Severity: LOW. Users implicitly share the workspace when
  debugging, but the current behavior removes the choice.

### F2 [LOW] tracing fields log operator-private paths
- Multiple `tracing::[info|warn|debug]!(..., path = %X.display(), ...)`
  call sites scatter socket paths, DB paths, and workspace paths
  through structured logs. Representative hits (non-exhaustive):
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/native_events.rs:258, 302, 331,
      362, 401 — unix socket paths.
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/telemetry.rs:1118 —
      `info_span!("telemetry_store_open", path = %db_path.display())`.
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs:1375, 2658, 2814 —
      socket paths on accept failures.
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/build_coord.rs:265 —
      `meta_path = %self.meta_path.display()`.
- Severity: LOW. These logs land in the user's local log file,
  not network-visible by default, but they do end up in diag
  bundles when `collect_recent_log_lines` is called.

## Non-findings verified in the same pass
- Bearer tokens in structured logs: grep across
  `tracing::{info,debug,warn,error}!` call sites for
  `token|bearer|secret|password|api_key` returned zero production
  hits. Clean.
- Pane content in tracing fields: no production code logs
  `segment.content`, `delta_text`, or `pane_text`. Only test
  `assert_eq!(segment.content, ...)` references found. Clean.
- Diag bundle reading privileged files: no `/etc/`, `.ssh/`,
  `.aws/`, `.gnupg/` reads in `diagnostic.rs`. The bundle only
  reads the ft SQLite DB (already redacted via `redact_events`)
  and writes to the workspace-local `diag_dir`. Clean.
- Environment variables in diag bundle: `gather_environment`
  captures only OS / arch / rust_version / wa_version / cwd —
  no `std::env::vars()` dump. Clean.
- Summarize_config: the docstring already calls out "no paths or
  secrets" and the implementation confirms — only numeric/boolean
  config values are copied over. Clean.

## Fix direction
- F1: replace absolute cwd with just the basename, or gate
  inclusion behind `DiagnosticOptions.include_cwd: bool` default
  `false`. Concretely at diagnostic.rs:87:
  ```rust
  cwd: if opts.include_cwd {
      std::env::current_dir().ok().map(|p| p.display().to_string())
  } else {
      std::env::current_dir()
          .ok()
          .and_then(|p| p.file_name().map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().into_owned()))
  },
  ```
- F2: when a log line is included in a diag bundle via
  `collect_recent_log_lines` (or equivalent), run it through the
  existing `Redactor` path-aware rules. Non-bundle in-process logs
  stay as-is since they aid operator debugging on the local box.

## Severity rationale
- LOW (P2): neither finding reveals a secret, and both sit behind
  an operator action (`ft diag bundle` + sharing it). The fix
  shrinks the unintentional-share footprint but doesn't close a
  live exploit.

## Related
- ft-ybtyg (598a7c13) — snapshot/checkpoint hashing honesty.
- ft-2guux (doc reality-check) — complementary; this bead is the
  diagnostics-exfil counterpart.

 {"id":"ft-ltpog","title":"native_events: decode errors logged at debug, invisible in production","description":"# [MEDIUM] native_events: malformed wire events logged at debug

**Subsystem:** native_events / protocol_recovery
**File:** crates/frankenterm-core/src/native_events.rs (`handle_connection_with_cx`)

## The hole

When wire-event decoding fails (invalid JSON, bad base64, unknown event
type), the handler emitted:

\`\`\`rust
Err(err) => {
    debug!(error = %err, "failed to decode native event (cx path)");
}
\`\`\`

A malformed wire event is a protocol anomaly — version skew between
frankenterm and the mux-server, on-wire corruption, or a hostile peer
writing into the native-events socket. Operators routinely filter
`debug!` in production, so the signal was effectively lost.

Contrast with the `MAX_EVENT_LINE_BYTES` path a few lines above, which
already emits at `warn!`. Same class of anomaly, inconsistent level.

## Fix

Promoted the decode-error log to `warn!`. The `warn!` import was already
present — no dependency change.

Future work (separate bead): attach a telemetry counter for
decode-failure rate so it can drive a SLI rather than relying solely on
log sampling.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:26:39.950825Z","created_by":"p3-review-audit","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:46:57.568904Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:46:57.568595Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["native-events","observability"]}

 {"id":"ft-ptjlp","title":"connector_outbound_bridge: dedup uses OutboundEvent.timestamp_ms as clock — same replay-bypass shape as ft-dijpe (inbound)","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_outbound_bridge.rs:1027` in `process_event`:

```rust
if event.correlation_id.is_some()
    && !self
        .deduplicator
        .check_and_record(&correlation_id, event.timestamp_ms)
{ ... }
```

And line 1080:

```rust
let now_ms = event.timestamp_ms;
```

Both use `event.timestamp_ms` as the dedup/processing clock. `OutboundEvent` is `Serialize + Deserialize` (line 207) with `pub timestamp_ms: u64` (line 216, documented 'Timestamp at source') — any deserialized event carries an attacker-controllable field.

## Shape

Identical to ft-dijpe (connector_inbound_bridge, closed in 0716e112). An OutboundEvent with:

1. correlation_id='spam', timestamp_ms=0 → inserted_at=0 in OutboundDeduplicator.
2. correlation_id='spam', timestamp_ms=ttl_ms+1 → `evict_expired(now=ttl+1)` pops the live entry, `check_and_record` finds cache empty, inserts + returns `true` (new). Replay accepted.

Repeat indefinitely by monotonically increasing the claimed timestamp. Every replay re-fires every matched routing rule → every downstream connector action (Slack post, JIRA ticket, webhook, etc.) fires again without bound.

## Severity

MEDIUM — same as inbound. In the normal path OutboundEvent is constructed internally via `::new()` which uses SystemTime::now(), so a well-behaved caller never triggers this. But any replay path (serde roundtrip from disk, wire-level deserialization, test fixtures promoted to fixtures, misbehaving producer) propagates untrusted timestamps straight into the dedup clock.

## Fix

Mirror ft-dijpe: replace `event.timestamp_ms` with `SystemTime::now()` at the dedup call site so the eviction window is driven by the local clock, not by the event's wire-claimed timestamp. Leave `let now_ms = event.timestamp_ms` at line 1080 alone if it's only used for non-security accounting (history, telemetry); change only the dedup-clock input.

## Test

`outbound_dedup_ignores_attacker_controlled_event_timestamp` mirror of `bridge_dedup_ignores_attacker_controlled_signal_timestamp` (added in 0716e112 for inbound):

* Route event A with cid='replay', timestamp_ms=0 → not deduplicated.
* Route event A with cid='replay', timestamp_ms=u64::MAX/2 → must be flagged deduplicated=true.

Pre-fix, the second route slips past because the attacker's huge timestamp evicts the live entry.  {"id":"ft-xdt8j","title":"[bug][resource-types] backpressure_severity propagates NaN through clamp() — observe_ratio(NaN) corrupts smoothed_ratio; from_severity(NaN) leaks NaN through every output field","description":"Discovered via proptest in frankenterm-core-resource-types (AmberHare 2026-04-26). Two related defects:

1. ContinuousBackpressure::observe_ratio (backpressure_severity.rs:140) calls raw_ratio.clamp(0.0, 1.0). Rust's f64::clamp returns NaN for NaN input — it does NOT map NaN to a saturation bound. Folding NaN into the EMA poisons smoothed_ratio() permanently (NaN never decays). severity() still survives because sigmoid() has explicit NaN→0.5 handling, but operator-facing readouts (smoothed_ratio observability) become NaN.

2. ThrottleActions::from_severity (backpressure_severity.rs:88) does s = severity.clamp(0.0, 1.0); for NaN input s is NaN. Then s*s, 0.25*s, 1.0+3.0*s, 1.0-0.8*s are all NaN. Every output field violates its documented [0,1]/[1,4]/etc. range.

Reproducer: cargo test -p frankenterm-core-resource-types --test proptest_backpressure_severity -- continuous_observe_keeps_invariants throttle_actions_clamps_every_output (currently the proptest avoids NaN inputs to keep CI green; un-skip by re-adding Just(f64::NAN) to arb_ratio_sequence/arb_severity_adversarial).

Recommended fix: replace .clamp(0.0, 1.0) with an explicit NaN-aware clamp:
  let safe = if x.is_nan() { 0.0 } else { x.clamp(0.0, 1.0) };
in both observe_ratio and from_severity. Same pattern is already present in sigmoid() (returns 0.5 for NaN), so the cluster is partly hardened but not consistently.

Severity: P2 — operator dashboards can display NaN; throttling logic is unaffected because sigmoid clamps.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:10:08.091800Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:20:53.074740Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T22:20:53.074204Z","close_reason":"Fixed in ec0160d6: explicit is_nan() guard in observe_ratio and from_severity maps NaN to least-throttled (0.0). Companion proptest re-enables NaN in adversarial strategies — 7/7 proptests + 97/97 lib tests green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-xgs7n","title":"[testing-fuzzing] pin extract_delta overlap-existence invariant: previous.ends_with(current[..len-delta.len()])","description":"# Gap

proptest_ingest_extract_delta_fuzz.rs covers 5 MRs (crash-freedom,
identity, content-suffix, pure-append, determinism) + 2 inline
regression tests, but does NOT pin the primary semantic contract of
the algorithm: **when Content(delta) is returned, the non-delta head
of `current` must be a tail of `previous`**. Formally:

    extract_delta(previous, current, _) = Content(delta)
    ⇒ previous.ends_with(&current[..current.len() - delta.len()])

This is the invariant the sliding-window search ACTUALLY proves
(search_window[pos..] == current[..overlap_len], where
search_window = previous[search_start..], so previous ends with
current[..overlap_len]). Without it, the algorithm could return a
Content(delta) where the "non-delta prefix" is pure fabrication —
the existing MR3 suffix-check proves current ends with delta but
says nothing about the overlap being real.

Adding this test catches any regression in the overlap-candidate
acceptance logic (`search_window[pos..] == current[..overlap_len]`
on ingest.rs:1711) that accidentally accepts a non-matching window.

Fix: new proptest in proptest_ingest_extract_delta_fuzz.rs covering
every path (pure-append fast path + sliding-window search).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:04:40.819353Z","created_by":"pane3-ingest-mr","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:13:32.759851Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:13:32.759521Z","close_reason":"Landed in 5eb9f956 (fix(apc, backpressure, ingest): …, overlap-existence proptest) — extract_delta_content_overlap_exists_in_previous added to tests/proptest_ingest_extract_delta_fuzz.rs:207. Verified: 1 passed / 0 failed / 14 filtered out on --test proptest_ingest_extract_delta_fuzz (test run took 0.20s under 512 proptest cases). Pins the semantic contract that when Content(delta) returns, previous.ends_with(current[..current.len() - delta.len()]) — the 'overlap was real' claim that MR3's suffix-only check does not catch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ingest","proptest","testing"]}

 {"id":"ft-xkpy1","title":"[ux][P3] Robot + MCP error-hint audit follow-up (beyond MCP_ERR_POLICY)","description":"## Context

/ux-audit sweep of robot + MCP surfaces. Primary finding closed as a trivial ship: all 7 MCP_ERR_POLICY deny sites in mcp_tools.rs (6 McpToolError::new sites + 1 McpEnvelope in the mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation gate helper) previously passed None as hint — leaving MCP clients with 'Read denied by policy' and nowhere to go. Now all seven route through a shared POLICY_DENY_HINT const naming two actionable recovery paths (config.safety.rules for operator review, policy_denied_audit table for decision context).

Secondary findings not in that ship, each worth a separate pass:

## 1. Robot error-code coverage drift vs README

Defined in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:4549-4564:
  robot.invalid_args, robot.unknown_subcommand, robot.config_error,
  robot.fts_query_error, robot.approval_error, robot.pane_not_found,
  robot.reservation_conflict, robot.storage_error, robot.feature_not_available,
  robot.policy_denied, robot.timeout, robot.workflow_aborted,
  robot.workflow_error, robot.workflow_not_found, robot.not_implemented,
  robot.remote_text_unavailable

README.md §Error Handling (robot mode) lists robot.wezterm_not_running and robot.require_approval — neither is a defined ROBOT_ERR_* constant. Either the README is stale (likely) or those codes exist under a different name (possible). Audit the README table against the actual constants + per-subcommand emission paths, update whichever is drifted.

## 2. Robot-mode hint uniformity

Walk every  construction in main.rs. MCP_ERR_POLICY just got treated; robot.pane_not_found, robot.timeout, robot.feature_not_available, etc. may have similar 'no-hint' patterns. Check each, add actionable hints matching the /ux-audit test: 'say what to do, not just what went wrong.'

## 3. Format-flag claims (toon vs json)

README claims  saves '40-60% fewer tokens' for AI-to-AI use. Spot-verify: render a few representative envelopes in both formats, measure actual byte counts, confirm the claim holds within some band. If TOON is actually ~15% smaller on small envelopes, the marketing text should be tightened.

## 4. Success-response conciseness

Every MCP tool envelope carries mcp_version, version, ok, data, elapsed_ms, now — 6 top-level keys. For small data payloads (e.g. a 2-item events list) the envelope can be 3-4x the size of the actual data. Consider whether any of mcp_version / version / now are redundant for the client once an initialize handshake has happened; file a separate architectural bead if so.

## Acceptance Criteria per item

- Item 1: README drift closed (either codes renamed in docs, or missing constants added in main.rs), with an assertion test that every documented code exists as a const.
- Item 2: Every robot-mode error construction reviewed; at least one shared const (like POLICY_DENY_HINT) for recurring hint families.
- Item 3: Measurement + either README update or TOON serializer improvement if claim is wrong.
- Item 4: Design doc; no code change until the redundancy is confirmed.
